briefdescription.para: An object with no data fields.
A large variable amount of data.
A type definitions.
A record type definition.
A single value.
A multiple data field object where each data field is a simple variable of the same basic data type.
A multiple data field object where the data fields may be any combination of simple variables.
The object can be read.
The object can be written.
The object can be mapped to a TPDO.
The object can be mapped to an RPDO.
Read-only access.
Write-only access.
Read or write access.
Read or write on process input.
Read or write on process output.
Constant value.
Refuse read on scan.
Refuse write on download.
If a read access is performed for the object, the data is stored in a file.

detaileddescription.para: In this case, the object contains the filename, not the file contents.

briefdescription.para: If a write access is performed for the object, the data is stored in a file.

detaileddescription.para: In this case, the object contains the filename, not the file contents.

briefdescription.para: The lower limit of the object value is of the form $NODEID { "+" number }.
The upper limit of the object value is of the form $NODEID { "+" number }.
The default object value is of the form $NODEID { "+" number }.
The current object value is of the form $NODEID { "+" number }.
The current object value was explicitly set with the ParamaterValue attribute in the EDS/DCF file.

memberdef.name: LELY_CO_DEFINE_TYPE
memberdef.initializer: co_##b##_t co_obj_get_val_##c( \ const co_obj_t *obj, co_unsigned8_t subidx); \ size_t co_obj_set_val_##c( \ co_obj_t *obj, co_unsigned8_t subidx, co_##b##_t c);

memberdef.name: LELY_CO_DEFINE_TYPE
memberdef.initializer: co_##b##_t co_sub_get_val_##c(const co_sub_t *sub); \ size_t co_sub_set_val_##c(co_sub_t *sub, co_##b##_t c);

memberdef.definition: typedef co_unsigned32_t co_sub_dn_ind_t(co_sub_t *sub, struct co_sdo_req *req, co_unsigned32_t ac, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_sub_dn_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen sub-object download indication function, invoked by an SDO download request or Receive-PDO indication.

detaileddescription.para: In case of an SDO request, this function is invoked for each segment.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request. The size, buf, nbyte and offset members of *req are set by the caller.
the SDO abort code, in case of an error during an SDO request.
a pointer to user-specified data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

memberdef.definition: typedef co_unsigned32_t co_sub_up_ind_t(const co_sub_t *sub, struct co_sdo_req *req, co_unsigned32_t ac, void *data)
memberdef.argsstring: (const co_sub_t *sub, struct co_sdo_req *req, co_unsigned32_t ac, void *data)
memberdef.name: co_sub_up_ind_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a CANopen sub-object upload indication function, invoked by an SDO upload request or Transmit-PDO indication.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object, containing the value.
a pointer to a CANopen SDO upload request. On the first invocation, the size member of *req is set to 0. All members MUST be initialized by the indication function.
the SDO abort code, in case of an error during an SDO request.
a pointer to user-specified data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a co_obj_t instance.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an uninitialized co_obj_t instance.
the object index.
pointer to the memory to be used by the object and its sub-objects to store values.
size of the memory.

simplesect.para: obj, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Finalizes a co_obj_t instance.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a valid co_obj_t instance.

briefdescription.para: Finds the previous object in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the previous object, or NULL if this is the first object.
co_dev_last_obj(), co_obj_next()

briefdescription.para: Finds the next object in the object dictionary of a CANopen device.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the next object, or NULL if this is the first object.
co_dev_first_obj(), co_obj_prev()

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the CANopen device containing the specified object.
Returns the index of a CANopen object.
Retrieves a list of sub-indices in a CANopen object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
the maximum number of sub-indices to return.
an array of at least maxsubidx indices (can be NULL). On success, *subidx contains the sub-indices.

simplesect.para: the total number of sub-indices in the object (which may be different from maxsubidx).

briefdescription.para: Inserts a sub-object into a CANopen object.

detaileddescription.para: This function fails if the sub-object is already part of another object, or of another sub-object with the same sub-index already exists.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
a pointer to the sub-object to be inserted.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error.
co_obj_remove_sub(), co_obj_find_sub()

briefdescription.para: Removes a sub-object from a CANopen object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
a pointer to the sub-object to be removed.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Finds a sub-object in a CANopen object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
the object sub-index.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the sub-object if found, or NULL if not.

briefdescription.para: Finds the first sub-object (with the lowest sub-index) in a CANopen object.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the first sub-object, or NULL if the object is empty.
co_dev_last_obj(), co_sub_next()

briefdescription.para: Finds the last sub-object (with the highest sub-index) in a CANopen object.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the last sub-object, or NULL if the object is empty.
co_obj_first_sub(), co_sub_prev()

briefdescription.para: Returns the name of a CANopen object.
Sets the name of a CANopen object.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the object code of a CANopen object.
Sets the code (type) of a CANopen object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
the object code (one of CO_OBJECT_NULL, CO_OBJECT_DOMAIN, CO_OBJECT_DEFTYPE, CO_OBJECT_DEFSTRUCT, CO_OBJECT_VAR, CO_OBJECT_ARRAY or CO_OBJECT_RECORD).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the value of a CANopen object.

detaileddescription.para: In case of compound data types, this is a pointer to the struct or array containing the members.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size (in bytes) of the value of a CANopen object.
Returns a pointer to the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of a pointer to the first byte in the array.

simplesect.para: co_obj_set_val(), co_sub_get_val()

memberdef.argsstring: (co_obj_t *obj, co_unsigned8_t subidx, const void *ptr, size_t n)
memberdef.name: co_obj_set_val

briefdescription.para: Sets the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
the object sub-index.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().
co_obj_get_val(), co_sub_set_val()

briefdescription.para: Sets the download indication function for a CANopen object.

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes co_sub_set_dn_ind() for all sub-objects.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
a pointer to the indication function. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Sets the upload indication function for a CANopen object.

detaileddescription.para: This function invokes co_sub_set_up_ind() for all sub-objects.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen object.
a pointer to the indication function. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used.
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a co_sub_t instance.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an uninitialized co_sub_t instance.
the object sub-index.
the data type of the sub-object value (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
pointer to the memory to be used by sub-object to store its value.

simplesect.para: sub, or NULL on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Finalizes a co_sub_t instance.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a valid co_sub_t instance.

briefdescription.para: Finds the previous sub-object in a CANopen object.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the previous sub-object, or NULL if this is the first sub-object.
co_obj_last_sub(), co_sub_next()

briefdescription.para: Finds the next sub-object in a CANopen object.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the next sub-object, or NULL if this is the first sub-object.
co_obj_first_sub(), co_sub_prev()

briefdescription.para: Returns the a pointer to the CANopen object containing the specified sub-object.
Returns the sub-index of a CANopen sub-object.
Returns the name of a CANopen sub-object.
Sets the name of a CANopen sub-object.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the data type of a CANopen sub-object.
Returns the address of the lower limit of the value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size (in bytes) of the lower limit of the value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the number of bytes in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the lower limit of the value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of a pointer to the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Sets the lower limit of a value of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the upper limit of the value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size (in bytes) of the upper limit of the value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the number of bytes in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the upper limit of the value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of a pointer to the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Sets the upper limit of a value of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the default value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size (in bytes) of the default value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the number of bytes in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the default value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of a pointer to the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Sets the default value of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns the address of the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns the size (in bytes) of the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the number of bytes in the array.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: In the case of strings or domains, this is the address of a pointer to the first byte in the array.

briefdescription.para: Sets the current value of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the bytes to be copied. In case of strings or domains, ptr MUST point to the first byte in the array.
the number of bytes at ptr. In case of strings, n SHOULD exclude the terminating null byte(s).

simplesect.para: the number of bytes copied (i.e., n), or 0 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Checks if the specifed value would be a valid value for a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: This function checks if the specified type matches that of the sub-object and, in the case of basic types, if the value is within range.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types.
a pointer to the value to be checked. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: Returns the access type of a CANopen sub-object.
Sets the access type of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
the access type (one of CO_ACCESS_RO, CO_ACCESS_WO, CO_ACCESS_RW or CO_ACCESS_CONST).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Returns true if it is possible to map the specified CANopen sub-object into a PDO, and false if not.
Enables or disables PDO mapping a CANopen sub-object.
Returns the object flags of a CANopen sub-object.
Sets the object flags of a CANopen sub-object.
Returns a pointer to the value of the UploadFile attribute of a CANopen sub-object, or NULL if the attribute does not exist.
Sets the value of the UploadFile attribute of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the null-terminated string to be copied to the UploadFile attribute.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().
the result of co_sub_get_flags() contains CO_OBJ_FLAGS_UPLOAD_FILE.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the value of the DownloadFile attribute of a CANopen sub-object, or NULL if the attribute does not exist.
Sets the value of the DownloadFile attribute of a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the null-terminated string to be copied to the DownloadFile attribute.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().
the result of co_sub_get_flags() contains CO_OBJ_FLAGS_DOWNLOAD_FILE.

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the download indication function for a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the download indication function for a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by co_sub_dn_ind().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the indication function. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_sub_on_dn()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when a download indication is received by a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: For a domain value with the CO_OBJ_FLAGS_DOWNLOAD_FILE flag set, this function invokes co_sdo_req_dn_file() to write the value to file. Otherwise the value is read from the SDO download request with co_sdo_req_dn_val() and, if it is within the specified range, written to the object dictionary with co_sub_dn().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request.
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 if all segments have been received and the value has been successfully written to the object dictionary, and -1 if one or more segments remain or an error has occurred. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: Invokes the download indication function of a CANopen sub-object, registered with co_sub_set_dn_ind().

detaileddescription.para: This is used for writing values to the object dictionary. If the indication function returns an error, or the refuse-write-on-download flag (CO_OBJ_FLAGS_WRITE) is set, the value of the sub-object is left untouched.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.

a pointer to a CANopen SDO download request. All members of *req, except membuf, MUST be set by the caller. The membuf MUST be initialized before the first invocation and MUST only be used by the indication function.
the SDO abort code.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

memberdef.argsstring: (co_sub_t *sub, co_unsigned16_t type, const void *val, struct membuf *buf)
memberdef.name: co_sub_dn_ind_val

briefdescription.para: Invokes the download indication function of a CANopen sub-object, registered with co_sub_set_dn_ind().

detaileddescription.para: This is used for writing values to the object dictionary. If the indication function returns an error, or the refuse-write-on-download flag (CO_OBJ_FLAGS_WRITE) is set, the value of the sub-object is left untouched.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
the data type (in the range [1..27]). This MUST be the object index of one of the static data types and SHOULD be the same as the return value of co_sub_get_type().
the address of the value to be written. In case of string or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer.
a pointer to the memory buffer used to store the serialized value. If NULL, an internal buffer is used.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: Downloads (moves) a value into a CANopen sub-object if the refuse-write-on-download flag (CO_OBJ_FLAGS_WRITE) is not set.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by the default download indication function.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the value to be written. In the case of strings or domains, this MUST be the address of pointer (which is set to NULL if the value is moved).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

briefdescription.para: Retrieves the upload indication function for a CANopen sub-object.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
the address at which to store a pointer to the indication function (can be NULL).
the address at which to store a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL).

briefdescription.para: Sets the upload indication function for a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: This function is invoked by co_sub_up_ind().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to the indication function. If ind is NULL, the default indication function will be used (which invokes co_sub_on_up()).
a pointer to user-specified data (can be NULL). data is passed as the last parameter to func.

briefdescription.para: Implements the default behavior when an upload indication is received by a CANopen sub-object.

detaileddescription.para: For a domain value with the CO_OBJ_FLAGS_UPLOAD_FILE flag set, this function invokes co_sdo_req_up_file() to read the value from file. Otherwise the value is read from the object dictionary with co_sub_get_val() and written to the SDO upload request with co_sdo_req_up_val().

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to a CANopen SDO upload request.
the address of a value which, on error, contains the SDO abort code (can be NULL).

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, *pac contains the SDO abort code.

briefdescription.para: Invokes the upload indication function of a CANopen sub-object, registered with co_sub_set_up_ind().

detaileddescription.para: This is used for reading values from the object dictionary.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CANopen sub-object.
a pointer to a CANopen SDO upload request. The size member of *req MUST be set to 0 on the first invocation. All members MUST be initialized by the indication function.
the SDO abort code.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, or an SDO abort code on error.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CANopen library; it contains the object dictionary declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
2021 Lely Industries N.V.
J. S. Seldenthuis jseldenthuis@lely.com
